home.title: Mark-Lee - The Ultimate Markdown Editor
home.meta_description: Focus on writing. Power in code. The desktop Markdown editor built for high performance.
home.hero_lead_text: A native Markdown editor for Windows that respects your RAM and your focus. Built with Rust's precision.
home.features_lead_text: Every feature is designed to accompany you, not distract you.
home.card_minimalism_desc: Focus on writing. The interface adapts, and tools appear only when you need them.
home.card_performance_desc: With a Rust backend, Mark-Lee is instant. No lags, no slowdowns.
home.card_customization_desc: Dozens of integrated and customizable themes for you to find your ideal environment.
home.card_pdf_desc: Transform your Markdowns into high-quality PDFs, ready for printing or sharing.
home.card_multiplatform_desc: Available for Windows, macOS, and Linux. Your favorite editor, anywhere.
home.card_dev_desc: Code block support, snippets, and deep integration with your workflow.
gallery.title: Gallery - Mark-Lee
gallery.hero_lead_text: Explore the beauty and functionality of Mark-Lee through visual themes and features.
theme.nord_desc: Cold elegance for coding and technical documentation.
theme.synthwave_desc: Vibrant, neon. For your boldest creations.
theme.forest_desc: Natural inspiration for calm, focused writing.
theme.coffee_desc: The coziness of a coffee shop. Ideal for long reads and prose.
theme.dark_desc: The classic dark mode, elegant and easy on the eyes.
theme.light_desc: Bright and crisp for well-lit environments.
feature.settings_desc: Customize shortcuts, appearance, and editor behavior.
feature.immersive_desc: Write without distractions, in full screen.
feature.pdf_desc: Generate professional PDFs with your formatting and style.
feature.focus_desc: Highlight only the paragraph you're currently working on.
feature.zen_desc: Interface disappears so you can focus entirely on the text.
development.title: Engineering - Mark-Lee
development.hero_lead_text: Unveiling the principles behind Mark-Lee: performance, minimalism, and security.
development.rust_tauri_desc: The native heart of Mark-Lee beats with Rust, a language offering unparalleled performance and memory safety, while Tauri allows us to build a lightweight and efficient desktop application using the system's WebView. This results in small binaries and low resource consumption.
development.react_ts_desc: The user interface is built with the latest React and TypeScript. React provides a fluid and reactive UI experience, while TypeScript ensures frontend code robustness and scalability with strong typing.
development.vite_tailwind_desc: For the development environment, we use Vite for ultra-fast HMR (Hot Module Replacement). TailwindCSS allows us to build the interface efficiently and modularly, with a consistent and optimized design.
development.webview2_desc: A Microsoft component that renders web content (Mark-Lee's interface) using the native Edge (Chromium) engine, ensuring performance and integration without packaging a full browser.
development.roadmap_v100_desc: New Themes: Nord, Synthwave, Forest, Coffee. Professional PDF Export: A4 layout, clean page breaks and typography. Focus Modes: Focus (overlay), Immersive (F11). Customizable Shortcuts: Define any key for actions. Snippets Manager: Quick insertion of tables, code blocks. UI/UX Improvements: Window dragging, modification indicator, auto-hide scrollbar and menu bar, auto-save and reading time.
development.roadmap_v050_desc: Editor Improvements: Selection counter (words/characters), Focus Mode (spotlight). More Features: HTML Export, Modification indicator. Enhanced UX: Configurable auto-save, Reading Time, Translations.
development.roadmap_v040_desc: New Features: Settings persistence, Recent files, Support for multiple file formats. UI/Core Improvements: Terminal theme, Auto-hide menu bar and scrollbar, Window transparency, Unsaved changes confirmation, Single window mode.
development.roadmap_v030_desc: UI Improvements: Window dragging, title adjustment, view mode indicators. File Association: Fix for opening via file association on Windows.
development.roadmap_next_desc: Implementation of a plugin system to allow the community to extend Mark-Lee's functionalities with custom resources and external integrations.
development.roadmap_future_desc: Exploration of P2P synchronization and real-time collaboration features, focusing on document security and privacy.
contributing.title: How to Contribute - Mark-Lee
contributing.hero_lead_text: Mark-Lee is an open-source project and welcomes your contribution.
contributing.bugs_desc: Found an error? Help us by reporting it on GitHub. Be as detailed as possible.
contributing.features_desc: Have an idea to improve Mark-Lee? Open an issue or discussion with your suggestion.
contributing.prs_desc: If you are a developer, feel free to submit a PR with improvements or new features.
contributing.guide_lead_text: To ensure a smooth and effective contribution process, follow these guidelines:
contributing.guide_setup_desc: Follow the 'Getting Started' guide in the project README to configure your development environment.
contributing.guide_git_desc: Create a separate branch for your changes (e.g., `feature/new-feature` or `fix/bug-name`). Make atomic commits with clear messages.
contributing.guide_code_desc: Maintain consistency with existing code style. If it's a new feature, include tests where applicable.
tech.rust_lead_text: The heart of Mark-Lee beats with the performance and security that only Rust can offer, packaged for the desktop with the efficiency of Tauri.
tech.rust_why_rust_desc: Rust is a programming language focused on performance and memory safety. It allows us to build a backend for Mark-Lee that is fast, reliable, and avoids entire classes of common bugs in other languages. Its promise of "zero-cost abstractions" means you don't pay for what you don't use. **Application in Mark-Lee:** Rust is used for all critical system operations, such as direct file system access (reading/writing documents), window management, and communication with the React frontend, ensuring the editor responds instantly and robustly.
tech.rust_why_tauri_desc: Tauri is a framework for building cross-platform desktop applications using web technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) for the user interface, but with a Rust backend. Unlike alternatives like Electron, Tauri uses the operating system's native WebView (WebView2 on Windows, WebKit on macOS/Linux), resulting in significantly smaller binaries and much lower RAM consumption. For Mark-Lee, this means an installer of only ~3MB and a more agile user experience. **Application in Mark-Lee:** Tauri allows the rich React interface to run in a native environment, with access to OS APIs that a common web application would not have, such as opening native file dialogs or managing borderless windows.
tech.rust_code_example_desc: This example shows how the React frontend invokes a Rust function (`save_document`) via Tauri to perform file system operations securely and efficiently, a vital integration for a desktop editor.
tech.react_lead_text: A fluid and reactive user experience, built on the robustness and scalability of React with TypeScript.
tech.react_why_react_desc: React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces. It enables declarative component development, where each component manages its own state, making the construction of complex UIs simpler and easier to maintain. In Mark-Lee, React is the foundation for the Markdown editor, real-time preview, menus, and modals. **Application in Mark-Lee:** React is fundamental to the editor's modularity. Each part of the interface, such as the toolbar, text editor, preview panel, and settings modals, is a React component. This facilitates maintenance, adding new features, and ensuring a responsive and real-time updated UI.
tech.react_why_ts_desc: TypeScript is a typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to plain JavaScript. It adds optional static typing to the code, which helps detect errors during development, improves readability, and facilitates refactoring, especially in large projects like Mark-Lee. Strong typing ensures that interactions between the frontend (React) and backend (Tauri/Rust) are secure and well-defined. **Application in Mark-Lee:** TypeScript is crucial for code quality. It allows us to define clear contracts for React component properties, application states, and data structures exchanged with the Rust backend. This minimizes bugs, improves collaboration, and makes developing new features faster and safer.
tech.react_code_example_desc: This example shows a typed React component for the editor, highlighting how state and props are managed clearly and securely for a fluid editing experience.
tech.vite_tailwind_lead_text: Modern tools that optimize Mark-Lee's development, ensuring fast builds and a responsive design.
tech.vite_why_vite_desc: Vite is a next-generation bundler for JavaScript/TypeScript projects. It offers an ultra-fast development server with instant HMR (Hot Module Replacement), which drastically speeds up the development process of Mark-Lee's interface. In the build phase, Vite optimizes the code for production, resulting in an extremely performant and lightweight web application, ideal for embedding in a Tauri application. **Application in Mark-Lee:** Vite is essential for development agility. It compiles the React frontend in seconds and allows immediate viewing of UI code changes, making prototyping and debugging much more efficient.
tech.vite_why_tailwind_desc: TailwindCSS is a utility-first CSS framework. Instead of writing traditional CSS, we use utility classes directly in the HTML to style components. This allows faster UI development, a responsive and consistent design, and a much smaller final CSS, as only used classes are included in the bundle. **Application in Mark-Lee:** TailwindCSS is used to style every component of Mark-Lee's interface, from buttons and toolbars to visual themes. Its flexibility allows for highly customizable design and maintains visual consistency across all application elements, resulting in a polished and professional UI.
tech.vite_code_example_title: Code Example (Mark-Lee UI Component with TailwindCSS)
tech.vite_code_example_desc: This example shows how TailwindCSS classes (`bg-gray-900`, `p-8`, `rounded-xl`, `shadow-lg`, `border`, `hover:bg-gray-800`, `transform`, `hover:-translate-y-1`) are combined to create a visually appealing and interactive UI element, such as the feature cards present on this site.
tech.webview2_lead_text: Cutting-edge web performance integrated into your desktop, without the overhead of a full browser.
tech.webview2_what_desc: WebView2 is a Microsoft component that allows embedding web content (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) in native applications, using the Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) rendering engine. In the context of Tauri and Mark-Lee, WebView2 is crucial because it is the "browser" that displays the user interface built with React, without the need to package a complete copy of Chrome (as Electron does). **Application in Mark-Lee:** Thanks to WebView2, Mark-Lee can offer a rich and modern user interface, developed with web technologies, without sacrificing application performance or size. It ensures that UI rendering is efficient and native to the Windows system, contributing to the lightweight and responsive experience that Mark-Lee offers.
tech.webview2_advantages_desc: The main advantage of WebView2 is its lightness and native integration. It uses existing system resources, resulting in a much smaller application size and reduced memory and CPU consumption. This directly contributes to the "Mark-Lee" experience of a fast and efficient editor that does not overload your system. Furthermore, WebView2 benefits from the security and performance updates of the Edge browser itself, ensuring that Mark-Lee's rendering component is always up-to-date and secure, without the need for massive application updates.
tech.tailwind_lead_text: Build fast and flexible user interfaces with a utility-first CSS framework.
tech.tailwind_philosophy_desc: TailwindCSS stands out for its utility-first approach. Instead of creating complex semantic classes in separate CSS files, you apply utility classes directly in your HTML. This means less context switching between files, faster development, and consistent designs. For Mark-Lee, this allows the interface to be styled and adapted agilely, maintaining visual cohesion. **Application in Mark-Lee:** TailwindCSS is fundamental for the speed of Mark-Lee's UI design and development. It allows designers and developers to work closely, applying styles directly to React components. This results in a highly customizable and responsive interface, without the need to write complex CSS from scratch, optimizing the delivery time of new themes and visual features.
tech.tailwind_optimization_desc: One of Tailwind's great advantages is that it generates only the CSS you actually use in your project. This results in extremely small production CSS files, contributing to Mark-Lee's lightness and performance. Maintenance becomes easier as style changes are localized in components, minimizing side effects. **Benefit for Mark-Lee:** By keeping the CSS bundle small and the style code clean, TailwindCSS contributes to the promise of a lightweight and performant application. New themes and customizations are easily implemented and maintained, ensuring Mark-Lee is always visually appealing and efficient.
tech.tailwind_code_example_title: Code Example (Mark-Lee UI Component with TailwindCSS)
tech.tailwind_code_example_desc: This example demonstrates how TailwindCSS is used to create modern and elegant UI elements in Mark-Lee, combining utility classes for layout, color, border, and effects like `backdrop-blur-lg` for a glassmorphism look.
